data.title: Genuine Parts falls after earnings, business separation update
data.description:
Genuine Parts (GPC) fell in early trading on Tuesday after missing Q4 estimates and setting full-year guidance below expectations.

Sales were up 4.1% during the quarter to $6.0B. The improvement was attributed by the company to a 1.7% increase in comparable sales, a 1.5% benefit from acquisitions, and a net 0.9% favorable impact of foreign currency and other. Sales were higher than a year ago in the North America Automotive, International Automotive, and Industrial segments.

During the quarter, the company's gross profit was impacted by $160M of certain non-recurring charges, primarily related to expected credit losses on volume purchase rebates and other amounts due from a vendor that filed petitions for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection. In the same period of the prior year, the company incurred a charge of $62 million to write down certain existing inventory associated with a new global rebranding and relaunch of a key tool and equipment offering. Adjusting for those charges, adjusted gross profit as a percentage of sales was up 70 basis points to 37.6% of sales. Q4 non-GAAP EPS was reported at $1.55 vs. $1.82 consensus and $1.61 a year ago.

For the full year, Genuine Parts (GPC) sees total sales growth of 3% to 5.5% and adjusted diluted earnings per share of $7.50 to $8.00 (midpoint $7.75) vs. $7.64 consensus.

Genuine Parts Company (GPC) confirmed its intention to separate the company into two independent, publicly traded companies, one comprising its Automotive Parts Group (Global Automotive) and the other comprising its Industrial Parts Group (Global Industrial). The transaction, which is targeted for completion in the first quarter of 2027, is expected to qualify as a tax-free transaction for U.S. federal tax purposes for Genuine Parts Company shareholders. The company plans to host investor days in the second half of 2026 to discuss operational initiatives to accelerate growth and margin expansion at Global Automotive and to provide strategic goals for each business.

"Over the past decade, we established leading global footprints in attractive geographies, simplified our business mix and accelerated strategic investments to advance and differentiate our business," stated CEO Will Stengel. "Creating two focused, independent companies sharpens customer and market alignment, increases clarity and speed, simplifies operations, and enables disciplined, business-specific investments to unlock long-term value," he added.

Shares of Genuine Parts (GPC) were down 6.2% in premarket trading on Tuesday.

data.title: Waystar Non-GAAP EPS of $0.36 misses by $0.04, revenue of $303.5M beats by $8.71M
data.description:
• Waystar press release (WAY): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.36 misses by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $303.5M (+24.3% Y/Y) beats by $8.71M.
  • 1,391 clients contributed over $100,000 in LTM revenue, up 16% year-over-year
  • Net revenue retention rate (NRR) of 112%
  • Fourth quarter 2025 subscription revenue of $167.8 million, up 38% year-over-year
  • Fourth quarter 2025 volume-based revenue of $134.2 million, up 11% year-over-year
  • Fiscal year 2025 subscription revenue of $558.4 million, up 22% year-over-year
  • Fiscal year 2025 volume-based revenue of $534.8 million, up 11% year-over-year

  • As of February 17, 2026, Waystar provides the following guidance for its full fiscal year 2026.

    • Total revenue is expected to be between $1.274 billion and $1.294 billion vs $1.28B consensus
    • Adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $530 million and $540 million
    • Non-GAAP net income is expected to be between $317 million and $335 million
    • Diluted non-GAAP net income per share is expected to be between $1.59 and $1.68 vs $1.63 consensus
  • Shares +2.6% PM.

data.title: Sunoco LPEPS of $0.09, revenue of $8.6B misses by $760M
data.description:
• Sunoco LP press release (SUN): Q4 EPS of $0.09 may not be comparable to consensus of $0.07.
  • net income of $97 million, Adjusted EBITDA of $706 million excluding one-time transaction-related expenses, and Distributable Cash Flow, as adjusted, of $442 million
  • Revenue of $8.6B (+63.2% Y/Y)  misses by $760M.
  • Distributable Cash Flow, as adjusted, attributable to SUN for the fourth quarter of 2025 was $442 million compared to $261 million in the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: USA Compression Partners misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• USA Compression Partners press release (USAC): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.22 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $252.48M (+2.7% Y/Y) misses by $0.95M.
  • Net cash provided by operating activities was $139.5 million for fourth-quarter 2025, compared to $130.2 million for fourth-quarter 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA of $154.5 million for fourth-quarter 2025, compared to $155.5 million for fourth-quarter 2024.
  • Distributable Cash Flow of $103.2 million for fourth-quarter 2025, compared to $96.3 million for fourth-quarter 2024.
  • Distributable Cash Flow Coverage was 1.36x for fourth-quarter 2025, compared to 1.56x for fourth-quarter 2024.
  • Paid cash distribution of $0.525 per common unit for fourth-quarter 2025, consistent with fourth-quarter 2024.
  • Average revenue-generating horsepower of 3.58 million for fourth-quarter 2025, compared to 3.56 million for fourth-quarter 2024.
  • Average revenue per revenue-generating horsepower per month of $21.69 for fourth-quarter 2025, compared to $20.85 for fourth-quarter 2024.
  • Average horsepower utilization was 94.5% for fourth-quarter 2025, consistent with for fourth-quarter 2024.

data.title: General Mills slides after setting profit guidance lower
data.description:
General Mills (GIS) provided a financial update ahead of the food company's presentation at the Consumer Analyst Group of New York Conference. Chairman. 

For FY26, organic sales are now expected to be down 1.5% to 2.0%, compared to the previous range of down 1% to up 1%. Adjusted operating profit and adjusted diluted EPS are now expected to be down 16% to 20% in constant currency, compared to the previous range of down 10% to 15% in constant currency. Free cash flow conversion is still expected to be at least 95% of adjusted after-tax earnings.

Later today, CEO Jeff Harmening, CFO Kofi Bruce, and Group President of North America Retail and North America Pet Dana McNabb will outline the company's plans to drive sustainable growth by delivering greater remarkability through the company’s global brands. With its Accelerate strategy as the guide, General Mills (GIS) noted that it is investing behind remarkability as the primary lever to restore organic sales growth and drive long-term sustainable shareholder value. During its presentation at CAGNY, management will discuss topics such as delivering remarkable brand experiences, prioritizing consumer-centric innovation, and unleashing digital and technology capabilities.

In terms of shareholder returns, General Mills (GIS) said its sustainable business model creates shareholder value through net sales growth, margin expansion, cash conversion, and cash returns to shareholders. The company highlighted that it has a strong track record of converting earnings into free cash flow, enabling it to return more than $14 billion to shareholders in the form of dividends and share repurchases since fiscal 2019.

Shares of General Mills (GIS) fell 4.9% in premarket trading.
